items.hook: The Apollo 11 lunar landing mission is launched from Cape Kennedy in Florida, USA.
items.body: Apollo 11 was the American spaceflight that first landed humans on the Moon, and the fifth crewed mission of NASA's Apollo program. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: A classicist’s verdict on Nolan’s Odyssey: a soulful hero flatters our times as women and nuance pushed overboard
items.body: Matt Damon’s sensitive and repentant Odysseus might come as a surprise to Homer, likewise some significant omissions concerning the poem’s female characters

items.hook: The Art of Opposition by Courttia Newland review – piercing essays on culture and creativity
items.body:
The novelist issues a inspiring call for artists to exercise their autonomy in a world of gatekeepers

In 1988, the late Ghanaian writer and filmmaker Kwesi Owusu edited Storms of the Heart: An Anthology of Black Arts & Culture,

items.hook: Tiger in the Bush (1957) is a novel for children by Australian author Nan Chauncy, illustrated by Margaret Horder.
items.body: It won the Children's Book of the Year Award: Older Readers in 1958 and was selected by Queen Elizabeth, the Queen Mother as a present for Prince Charles and Princess Anne. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: ‘Cool, suave and weirdly shy’: Miranda Richardson remembers Sam Neill
items.body: Sam had such ease about him and I just loved the way he seemed to cruise through life. But he confronted his mortality with real courage

items.opener: Here's one for the group chat —
items.hook: Henry Morton Robinson was an American novelist, best known for A Skeleton Key to Finnegans Wake written with Joseph Campbell and his 1950 novel The Cardinal, which Time magazine reported was "The year's most popular book, fiction or nonfiction."
items.body: Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Unheard David Bowie songs from 1965 to be released – including ones with Jimmy Page on guitar
items.body:
Compilation entitled David Bowie: The Shel Talmy Recordings will be released in September, collating material from when he recorded as Davy Jones

Unheard 1965 recordings by David Bowie, from when he was starting out in swinging 60s

items.hook: He played college football for the South Dakota Coyotes.
items.body: Jack Cochrane is an American professional football linebacker for the Kansas City Chiefs of the National Football League (NFL). Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Following token resistance, the city of Nazareth, revered by Christians as the hometown of Jesus, capitulates to Israeli troops during Operation Dekel in the 1948 Arab–Israeli War.
items.body: Nazareth is the largest city in the Northern District of Israel. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Bernard the Brave is a novel written by British novelist Margery Sharp.
items.body: It is the eighth novel in a series of nine known collectively as The Rescuers which tells the story of two little mice, Bernard and Miss Bianca, and their adventures as members of the Mouse Prisoner's Aid Society, a mouse organization dedicated to cheering up

items.hook: South Vietnamese Colonel Phạm Ngọc Thảo, a formerly undetected communist spy and double agent, is hunted down and killed by unknown individuals after being sentenced to death in absentia for a February 1965 coup attempt against Nguyễn Khánh.
items.body: The Army of the Republic of Vietnam composed the ground forces of the South Vietnamese military from its inception in 1955 to the fall of Saigon on 30 April 1975. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Indonesia Malaise is a 1931 film directed by the Wong brothers.
items.body: It was the brothers' first sound film and one of the first such films in the Dutch East Indies. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.opener: Here's one for the group chat —
items.hook: The Blue Lagoon is a 1980 American drama film directed by Randal Kleiser from a screenplay written by Douglas Day Stewart based on the 1908 novel of the same name by Henry De Vere Stacpoole.
items.body: The film stars Brooke Shields and Christopher Atkins. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: The world's first parking meter is installed in Oklahoma City, Oklahoma.
items.body: A parking meter is a device used to collect money in exchange for the right to park a vehicle in a particular place for a limited amount of time. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Alf's Button is a 1930 British comedy film directed by W.
items.body: Kellino and starring Tubby Edlin, Alf Goddard and Nora Swinburne. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: The Spanish town of Arjona declares independence and names its native Muhammad ibn Yusuf as ruler. This marks the Muhammad's first rise to prominence; he later established the Nasrid Emirate of Granada, the last independent Muslim state in Spain.
items.body: Arjona is a municipality in the province of Jaén, Andalusia, Spain. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: John Robertson Duigan makes the first flight of the Duigan pusher biplane, the first aircraft built in Australia.
items.body: John Robertson Duigan MC was an Australian pioneer aviator who built and flew the first Australian-made aircraft. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Expedition Sound podcast series
items.body: Expedition Sound is a podcast series by the European Space Agency (ESA) in which you’re invited to explore the Universe through your ears. Guided by sonifications – the translation of data into sound – host Zsófi Szalavári travels from our

items.hook: Rebecca Perry wins Waterstones debut fiction prize for ‘delicious and dream-like’ novel
items.body:
May We Feed the King, about a museum curator and a medieval monarch, was praised by judges for its ‘crisp, cool prose’ and ‘poet’s eye for detail’

Author and poet Rebecca Perry has won the 2026 Waterstones debut fiction prize for M

items.hook: The Odyssey review – Nolan goes god-tier with breathtaking epic of men, monsters and moral metamorphosis
items.body: Doing full justice to the Homeric legend, Christopher Nolan amasses an epic cast to convey the true cost of war with film-making of thrilling ambition
